algorithms.title: Algorithms
algorithms.description: Explore algorithm visualizations and interactive simulations
algorithms.heroDescription: Explore cutting-edge algorithms through interactive visualizations. From machine learning transformers to quantum circuits, understand complex concepts with hands-on experimentation.
algorithms.noAlgorithmsHint: Try adjusting your filters or search query
algorithms.learningDescription: Each algorithm visualization includes step-by-step explanations, adjustable parameters, and real-time feedback. Experiment with different inputs to develop intuition for how these algorithms work. All visualizations are built with modern web technologies and are fully accessible.
algorithms.quantumDescription: Quantum circuits are the quantum analog of classical logic circuits. They consist of quantum gates applied to qubits, enabling operations that leverage superposition and entanglement. This simulator lets you build and visualize quantum circuits.

filter.title: Filter Algorithms
filter.searchAriaLabel: Search algorithms by name or description

astar.title: A* Search Algorithm
astar.description: A* (A-star) is an informed search algorithm that uses heuristics to find optimal paths efficiently. It combines the benefits of Dijkstra's algorithm (guaranteed optimality) with greedy best-first search (speed through heuristics), making it ideal for pathfinding in games and robotics.

dijkstra.title: Dijkstra's Shortest Path Algorithm
dijkstra.description: Dijkstra's algorithm finds the shortest path between nodes in a weighted graph. It uses a greedy approach with a priority queue to efficiently explore paths, guaranteeing optimal results for graphs with non-negative edge weights.

graphTraversal.title: Graph Traversal: BFS vs DFS
graphTraversal.description: Graph traversal algorithms systematically visit all vertices in a graph. Breadth-First Search (BFS) explores level by level using a queue, while Depth-First Search (DFS) dives deep using a stack. Both are fundamental to many graph algorithms and have distinct use cases.

mst.title: Minimum Spanning Tree (Kruskal's Algorithm)
mst.description: Kruskal's algorithm finds a minimum spanning tree (MST) for a weighted undirected graph. It uses a greedy approach: sort edges by weight, then add edges to the MST if they don't create a cycle. Uses the Union-Find data structure for efficient cycle detection.

pagerank.title: PageRank Algorithm
pagerank.description: PageRank is the algorithm that powered Google's early search engine. It assigns importance scores to web pages based on link structure, treating the web as a graph where links are edges. Pages linked by many important pages receive higher scores.

transformers.title: Transformer Attention
transformers.description: The transformer architecture revolutionized natural language processing and sequence modeling. At its heart is the self-attention mechanism, which allows the model to weigh the importance of different positions in a sequence when processing each element.

metaLearning.title: Meta-Learning (MAML)
metaLearning.description: Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning (MAML) trains models to quickly adapt to new tasks with minimal data. Instead of learning a specific task, MAML learns an initialization that can be rapidly fine-tuned.

zkp.title: Zero-Knowledge Proofs
zkp.description: Zero-knowledge proofs allow one party (the prover) to prove to another party (the verifier) that they know a value, without revealing any information about the value itself. This powerful cryptographic primitive enables privacy-preserving authentication and verification.
zkp.fullDescription: Zero-knowledge proofs allow one party (the prover) to prove to another party (the verifier) that they know a value, without revealing any information about the value itself. This page includes an interactive step-by-step protocol demo and a WebGPU-accelerated batch verifier that runs modular exponentiation in parallel across all rounds simultaneously.
zkp.webgpuDescription: The "GPU Acceleration" tab runs Schnorr batch verification entirely on the GPU using WebGPU compute shaders — parallelising modular exponentiation across all rounds simultaneously. Falls back to CPU automatically if unavailable.

transformers.title: Transformer Attention
transformers.description: Explore how transformers process sequences with self-attention mechanisms. Visualize query, key, and value matrices in real-time.

metaLearning.title: Meta-Learning (MAML)
metaLearning.description: Discover Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning and how models learn to learn. Train on tasks and adapt to new ones quickly.

zkp.title: Zero-Knowledge Proofs
zkp.description: Understand cryptographic protocols that prove knowledge without revealing information. Interactive Schnorr protocol demonstration.

quantum.title: Quantum Simulation
quantum.description: Build quantum circuits with gates like Hadamard, CNOT, and Toffoli. Visualize superposition and entanglement.

pagerank.title: PageRank Algorithm
pagerank.description: Explore Google's original ranking algorithm. Build graphs, adjust damping, and watch scores converge iteratively.

fourier.title: Fourier Analysis
fourier.description: Transform time-domain signals to frequency domain using FFT and DFT. Visualize magnitude and phase spectrums.

gameTheory.title: Game Theory
gameTheory.description: Analyze strategic interactions using Nash equilibrium. Explore classic games like Prisoner's Dilemma and Battle of the Sexes.

chaos.title: Chaos Theory
chaos.description: Visualize chaotic systems including Lorenz attractors and logistic maps. Explore strange attractors and bifurcation diagrams.

pde.title: PDE Solver
pde.description: Solve partial differential equations numerically. Simulate heat diffusion and wave propagation with finite difference methods.

pde3d.title: PDE Solver 3D
pde3d.description: Three-dimensional PDE visualization with isosurface rendering, slice planes, and point clouds. Solve Heat and Wave equations on voxel grids.

graphsFull.title: Graph Algorithms
graphsFull.description: Explore graph traversal and shortest path algorithms. Visualize BFS, DFS, Dijkstra, and A* on custom graphs.

mlAlgorithms.title: ML Algorithms
mlAlgorithms.description: Interactive visualizations of machine learning algorithms including neural networks, decision trees, and clustering.

symbolic.title: Symbolic Mathematics
symbolic.description: Symbolic differentiation and calculus operations with NextCalc Pro
symbolic.taylorSubtitle: Watch how polynomial approximations converge to transcendental functions, term by term. Explore Taylor and Maclaurin series with interactive controls and animated plots.
symbolic.taylorFormulaDesc: The Taylor series of a function f(x) around a point a is defined as:
symbolic.maclaurinNote: When a = 0 this is called a Maclaurin series. The approximation improves as more terms are added, and the series converges within the radius of convergence R of the function.

units.title: Unit Converter
units.description: Convert between 80+ units across 12 categories: length, mass, temperature, volume, area, speed, time, data, pressure, energy, force, and angle.
units.pageSubtitle: Convert between 80+ units across 12 measurement categories with high-precision, SI-based calculations.

category.massDescription: Weight and mass from milligrams to tons
category.areaDescription: Surface area from square millimeters to acres
category.dataDescription: Digital storage from bits to terabytes
category.energyDescription: Work and energy from joules to BTU
category.angleDescription: Angular measure in degrees and radians

stats.footerNote: All calculations run client-side for instant, private analysis

matrix.pageSubtitle: Linear algebra computations with full matrix support

complex.pageSubtitle: Arithmetic, functions, and Argand diagram visualization for complex numbers

plots.title: Function Plotter

plots.subtitle: GPU-accelerated mathematical visualization with real-time interactions

chaos.title: Chaos Theory
chaos.description: Explore Lorenz attractors, logistic maps, and bifurcation diagrams
chaos.subtitle: Explore strange attractors, bifurcations, and deterministic chaos
chaos.lorenzParametersDesc: Adjust the parameters of the Lorenz system
chaos.trajectory3dDesc: WebGL · HDR bloom · OrbitControls — drag, scroll or pinch to navigate
chaos.clickSimulate: Click simulate to generate the attractor
chaos.timeSeriesDesc: Canvas 2D · gradient fill · scroll/pinch to zoom · drag to pan
chaos.simulateToSee: Simulate to see the time series
chaos.bifurcationDesc: WebGPU compute-accelerated · pixel-rendered route to chaos · scroll/pinch to zoom · drag to pan
chaos.lorenzAbout: Discovered by Edward Lorenz in 1963 while studying weather patterns. This system exhibits sensitive dependence on initial conditions — the famous "butterfly effect" where tiny changes lead to vastly different outcomes.
chaos.strangeAttractorsAbout: Unlike regular attractors that settle to fixed points or cycles, strange attractors have fractal structure and never repeat exactly. They reveal the beautiful geometric structure underlying chaotic systems.
chaos.logisticAbout: A simple population model that demonstrates how complex, chaotic behaviour can emerge from simple deterministic rules. The bifurcation diagram shows the period-doubling route to chaos discovered by Mitchell Feigenbaum.
chaos.applicationsAbout: Chaos theory applies to weather prediction, stock markets, population dynamics, heart rhythms, neural networks, and the solar system's long-term stability. Understanding chaos is crucial for prediction in complex systems.

fourier.title: Fourier Analysis
fourier.description: FFT and DFT frequency analysis with interactive signal construction
fourier.subtitle: GPU-accelerated time-frequency domain analysis with interactive visualizations
fourier.algorithmComplexity: FFT: O(n log n) — DFT: O(n²) — switching re-runs analysis
fourier.generateOrInput: Generate or input a signal to visualize
fourier.fftAbout: The FFT is an efficient O(n log n) algorithm for computing the Discrete Fourier Transform. It's essential for real-time signal processing, audio analysis, and scientific computing.
fourier.applicationsAbout: Used in MP3 compression, image processing (JPEG), telecommunications, radar systems, earthquake analysis, and quantum computing algorithms.
fourier.frequencyResolutionAbout: The frequency resolution is determined by the sample rate divided by the number of samples. More samples provide better resolution but require more computation time.
fourier.nyquistTheoremAbout: To accurately reconstruct a signal, the sampling rate must be at least twice the highest frequency present in the signal (Nyquist frequency).

gameTheory.title: Game Theory
gameTheory.description: Nash equilibrium analysis and strategy visualization
gameTheory.subtitle: Analyze strategic interactions and find Nash equilibria
gameTheory.gameSetupDesc: Select a preset or configure your own matrix
gameTheory.mixedStrategyHint: This game may have a mixed strategy equilibrium not shown here
gameTheory.iesdsHint: Iterative elimination of dominated strategies (IESDS) simplifies the game and narrows down likely equilibrium outcomes.
gameTheory.nashAbout: Named after John Nash, a Nash equilibrium is a strategy profile where no player can unilaterally improve their payoff. It is the cornerstone of modern game theory, underpinning auction design, pricing strategy, and international relations.
gameTheory.prisonersDilemmaAbout: The archetypal example of how individual rationality produces collectively suboptimal outcomes. Both players have a dominant strategy to defect, yet mutual defection (1,1) is worse than mutual cooperation (3,3) — the defining tension of social dilemmas.
gameTheory.applicationsAbout: Game theory informs economics, political science, evolutionary biology, computer science, and military strategy. Key applications include mechanism design for auctions, voting system analysis, firm pricing decisions, and modelling arms races.
gameTheory.dominatedAbout: A strategy is strictly dominated if another strategy always yields a higher payoff regardless of what opponents do. Rational players never play dominated strategies, and iterative elimination (IESDS) often uniquely solves the game.
gameTheory.dominatedDesc: Strategies always worse than alternatives regardless of opponent play
gameTheory.nashDesc: Strategy profiles where no player can benefit by unilaterally deviating

pde.title: PDE Solver
pde.heatAbout: The heat equation describes how temperature distributes over time in a given space. It's a parabolic PDE that models diffusion processes - heat naturally flows from hot to cold regions until equilibrium is reached.
pde.waveAbout: The wave equation describes oscillations and vibrations. It's a hyperbolic PDE that models sound waves, electromagnetic waves, vibrating strings, and water waves. Unlike heat, waves propagate without dissipation.
pde.finiteDifferenceAbout: This numerical technique approximates derivatives using discrete grid points. We replace continuous derivatives with difference quotients, converting PDEs into systems of algebraic equations that computers can solve efficiently.
pde.applicationsAbout: PDEs are fundamental to physics and engineering: weather prediction, fluid dynamics, quantum mechanics, image processing, financial modeling (Black-Scholes), and materials science.

3d.title: PDE 3D Studio
3d.description: 3D PDE solver with isosurface, slice, and point cloud visualization

ml.title: Modern ML Algorithms
ml.subtitle: Explore contrastive learning and advanced attention mechanisms
